mapping.a_element: parameters of the Dark Dimension dark-matter model (graviton mass, time-dependent kick velocity)
mapping.b_element: physical parameters to be estimated (blockage shape, range, source directions of arrival)

mapping.a_element: cosmological observables (CMB, BAO, Stage-IV tomographic survey data)
likelihood-based constraint of the model parameters from the data
Stage-IV survey forecast of the parameter-constraining power (Fisher-matrix forecast)
mapping.b_element: Cramer-Rao bound (CRB) quantifying the fundamental estimation precision

mapping.a_element: survey depth / data quality that sets how tightly the parameters can be pinned down
mapping.b_element: signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) governing how closely the ML estimator approaches the CRB

brief_justification: Both are the same estimation-theoretic machinery: infer the parameters of a physical forward model from noisy observations and quantify the fundamental achievable precision via Fisher information / the Cramer-Rao bound. Paper A's 'forecast' of the dark-sector parameters for a Stage-IV survey is precisely a Fisher/CRB forecast, the same object Paper B derives explicitly (ML estimator approaching the CRB) for blockage and DoA sensing, so the estimation framework maps role-for-role despite the disjoint cosmology-vs-wireless domains.
